seoTitle: Stony Crossing vs Stotts Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stony Crossing and Stotts Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stony Crossing vs Stotts Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stony Crossing and Stotts Creek.
heading: Stony Crossing vs Stotts Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stony Crossing, New South Wales and Stotts Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Stotts Creek (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stony Crossing or Stotts Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stony Crossing has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Stotts Creek scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stony Crossing scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Stotts Creek scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stony Crossing or Stotts Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
